name: Veridian Platform
description: The core SaaS product: a workflow automation and orchestration platform designed for mid-market finance teams. Deployed as a multi-tenant cloud application on AWS. Current version: 4.2. Customers manage, automate, and audit their financial workflows entirely within the platform.
business_value: Primary revenue driver. All 89 active customers use it. ARR: £8.2M as of Q4 2025. NRR: 112% (up from 108% last year, recovering toward the 118% peak two years ago).

name: Hartfield Group
description: Enterprise customer. A UK-based asset management firm with £12B AUM. Uses Veridian for fund accounting workflow automation across 4 teams. Their Head of Finance (Richard Hartfield) is the primary executive sponsor. Technically sophisticated — their IT team has raised SSO as a security requirement repeatedly.
business_value: ARR: £184,000/year. Highest single-account ARR in portfolio. At-risk due to SSO commitment breach. If churned, represents 2.2% ARR loss and a likely negative case study given Richard Hartfield's industry connections.

name: Meridian Capital
description: Enterprise customer. A mid-market private equity firm using Veridian for portfolio company reporting automation. Currently expanding their use case from 2 teams to 5, which triggered the contract upsell from £84K to £140K ARR. Their Finance Director is enthusiastic about the platform.
business_value: ARR: £84,000/year, expanding to £140,000 on renewal. Expansion customer — high retention probability. Two untracked commitments (custom dashboard, CSV export) are latent risks at renewal.

name: Solaris Financial
description: Enterprise customer. A specialist financial services firm (wealth management + corporate treasury). Recently renewed at a 15% uplift. The dedicated CSM commitment was the deciding factor in their renewal. Currently being served by Chloe Beaumont on a temporary basis while a new CSM is hired.
business_value: ARR: £96,000/year (renewed at 15% uplift from £83,500). Stable. Risk: if dedicated CSM is not in place soon, renewal goodwill may erode.

name: Finance Workflow Engine
description: The core product module that powers financial workflow automation — reconciliation rules, approval chains, audit trails, and GL mapping. The highest-complexity and highest-revenue component of the Veridian Platform. 80% of customer workflows run through this module.
business_value: Powers the workflows that drive 80%+ of customer value. Technical debt in this module was a primary driver of the Q1 2026 engineering freeze. Test coverage at 34% (target: 70% by end of Q1 2026). Any instability here directly threatens top-tier customer accounts.

name: Customer Data Platform
description: Internal data infrastructure built on BigQuery + dbt + Looker. Aggregates customer usage data, product telemetry, Salesforce CRM data, and Intercom support data into a single analytical layer. Used by CS for account health scoring and by Product for usage analytics.
business_value: Enables data-driven account health scoring, usage-based expansion signals, and the Looker dashboards that CS uses in renewal and QBR conversations. Data quality issues in this platform directly degrade CS effectiveness.

name: Engineering Runbook Library
description: A Notion-hosted collection of operational runbooks for Engineering incident response, deployment procedures, and infrastructure operations. Currently covers approximately 60% of known incident types. Identified as a critical gap in the Q1 2026 tech debt freeze plan.
business_value: Reduces MTTR and on-call burden. Incomplete coverage means incidents take longer to resolve and knowledge is trapped in senior engineers' heads. Building this out is a Q1 2026 commitment.

name: Series B Funding
description: The £38M Series B raised in November 2023. Investors: Northwood Ventures (lead), Apex Capital (follow-on). Remaining runway approximately 28 months as of Q1 2026. The company is targeting Series C in H2 2026 — needs a £12M+ ARR narrative.
business_value: Primary financial resource constraining all major hiring and investment decisions. Burn rate: approximately £980K/month (higher than Series B baseline due to 150-person headcount). Series C timeline is directly linked to ARR growth — current £8.2M ARR trajectory needs to show £12M+ ARR to support a credible Series C narrative.

name: Sales Commitment Log
description: Informal record of customer commitments made during sales cycles. Currently maintained as a personal Notion document by Chloe Beaumont in CS — not a company-owned system. Incomplete and not integrated with Salesforce or Linear.
business_value: This is a critical institutional memory gap. Three customer commitments (com-001, com-002, com-011) were either made without Engineering knowledge or tracked only informally. A proper commitment tracking system was scoped (dec-009) but deferred. This document is a single point of failure.
